prevalence.value: 5-15% (toddlers; higher in low-income groups and migrants)

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Low hemoglobin with microcytosis; ferritin and TSAT confirm; evaluate diet and blood loss

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: variable; screening policies differ by member state
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High with supplementation and dietary counseling

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care access is high; lab testing availability is strong
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Oral iron supplementation and dietary counseling; follow-up response testing
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Ensuring screening and follow-up in vulnerable groups
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Robust primary care and nutrition support programs in many member states
